description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crab, featuring a broad, oval-shaped carapace with a slightly domed surface and subtle mottling in shades of brown, tan, and orange. The crab has two prominent claws (chelae) at the front, each robust and slightly curved, with visible segmentation and texture. Its body is flanked by four pairs of walking legs on each side, each jointed and tapering to a point, with a slightly rough, matte finish. The dorsal surface of the carapace is hard and appears slightly rough, with small bumps and natural irregularities. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front corners of the carapace, and the mouthparts are tucked beneath the front edge. The overall appearance suggests a cooked or preserved specimen, with coloration and texture typical of edible crabs found in urban seafood markets or restaurants.
description: A lifelike crab with a broad, mottled carapace, two large claws, and eight jointed legs, displaying natural brown and orange hues with a hard, textured shell.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the carapace, both claws extended forward, and the eyes on short stalks clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the curvature of the carapace, the segmentation of the legs, and the robust structure of the nearest claw.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back edge of the carapace, the symmetrical arrangement of the legs, and the claws partially visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the other set of legs and claw, with the carapace's surface texture and color gradation more apparent.
